reasoning: The most recent known value of the TGA was unusually high around $905B. The Treasury generally targets an end-of-quarter TGA balance around $750B to $850B. Because the balance is currently elevated above typical targets, the Treasury gives itself room to draw down the balances over the subsequent months. Although early November outlays will likely drag the November 9th balance slightly down from $905B, by early February (ahead of tax season but during a period when outlays start to outpace receipts) the balance is still more likely to have reverted closer to its lower target than to have increased further from its early November value. Consequently, a decrease is slightly more probable over this horizon.
